heading: Protection of health and safety during disasters
label: 300hh–14 Protection of health and safety during disasters
notes: References in TextSection 264(c) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996, referred to in subsec. (b)(7), is , which is set out as a note under .section 264(c) of Pub. L. 104–191section 1320d–2 of this title

CodificationSection was enacted as part of the Security and Accountability For Every Port Act of 2006, also known as the SAFE Port Act, and not as part of the Public Health Service Act which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: in which a participating responder is a participant as a condition of the employment of such participating responder; and

children.label: B
children.text: that the Secretary of Health and Human Services certifies includes an adequate baseline medical screening.

children.heading: Disaster area
children.label: 2 Disaster area
children.text: The term “disaster area” means an area in which the President has declared a major disaster (as that term is defined in ), during the period of such declaration.section 5122 of this title

children.heading: High exposure level
children.label: 3 High exposure level
children.text: The term “high exposure level” means a level of exposure to a substance of concern that is for such a duration, or of such a magnitude, that adverse effects on human health can be reasonably expected to occur, as determined by the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, in accordance with human monitoring or environmental or other appropriate indicators.

children.label: i
children.text: a police officer;

children.label: ii
children.text: a firefighter;

children.label: iii
children.text: an emergency medical technician;

children.label: iv
children.text: any participating member of an urban search and rescue team; and

children.label: v
children.text: any other relief or rescue worker or volunteer that the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, determines to be appropriate;

children.label: B
children.text: a worker who responds to a disaster, either natural or manmade, involving any mode of transportation in the United States or disrupting the transportation system of the United States, by assisting in the cleanup or restoration of critical infrastructure in and around a disaster area;

children.label: C
children.text: a person whose place of residence is in a disaster area, caused by either a natural or manmade disaster involving any mode of transportation in the United States or disrupting the transportation system of the United States;

children.label: D
children.text: a person who is employed in or attends school, child care, or adult day care in a building located in a disaster area, caused by either a natural or manmade disaster involving any mode of transportation in the United States or disrupting the transportation system of the United States, of the United States; and

children.label: E
children.text: any other person that the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, determines to be appropriate.

children.heading: Participating responder
children.label: 5 Participating responder
children.text: The term “participating responder” means an individual described in paragraph (4)(A).

children.heading: Program
children.label: 6 Program
children.text: The term “program” means a program described in subsection (b) that is carried out for a disaster area.

children.heading: Substance of concern
children.label: 7 Substance of concern
children.text: The term “substance of concern” means a chemical or other substance that is associated with potential acute or chronic human health effects, the risk of exposure to which could potentially be increased as the result of a disaster, as determined by the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, and in coordination with the Agency for Toxic Substances and Disease Registry, the Environmental Protection Agency, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, the National Institutes of Health, the Federal Emergency Management Agency, the Occupational Health and Safety Administration, and other agencies.

children.label: A
children.text: the individuals are adequately informed about and protected against potential health impacts of any substance of concern in a timely manner;

children.label: i
children.text: any short- and long-term health impacts of any substance of concern; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any mental health impacts;

children.label: C
children.text: the individuals receive health care referrals as needed and appropriate; and

children.label: D
children.text: information from any such monitoring and studies is used to prevent or protect against similar health impacts from future disasters.

children.label: A
children.text: collecting and analyzing environmental exposure data;

children.label: B
children.text: developing and disseminating information and educational materials;

children.label: C
children.text: performing baseline and followup clinical health and mental health examinations and taking biological samples;

children.label: D
children.text: establishing and maintaining an exposure registry;

children.label: E
children.text: studying the short- and long-term human health impacts of any exposures through epidemiological and other health studies; and

children.label: F
children.text: providing assistance to individuals in determining eligibility for health coverage and identifying appropriate health services.

children.heading: Timing
children.label: 3 Timing
children.text: To the maximum extent practicable, activities under any program carried out under paragraph (1) (including baseline health examinations) shall be commenced in a timely manner that will ensure the highest level of public health protection and effective monitoring.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Participation in any registry or study that is part of a program carried out under paragraph (1) shall be voluntary.

children.heading: Protection of privacy
children.label: B Protection of privacy
children.text: The President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall take appropriate measures to protect the privacy of any participant in a registry or study described in subparagraph (A).

children.heading: In general
children.label: i In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall give priority in any registry or study described in subparagraph (A) to the protection, monitoring and study of the health and safety of individuals with the highest level of exposure to a substance of concern.

children.heading: Modifications
children.label: ii Modifications
children.text: Notwithstanding clause (i), the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, may modify the priority of a registry or study described in subparagraph (A), if the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, determines such modification to be appropriate.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, may carry out a program under paragraph (1) through a cooperative agreement with a medical institution, including a local health department, or a consortium of medical institutions.

children.label: I
children.text: the disaster area with respect to which the program is carried out; and

children.label: II
children.text: any other area in which there reside groups of individuals that worked or volunteered in response to the disaster; and

children.label: I
children.text: developing clinical protocols and conducting clinical health examinations, including mental health assessments;

children.label: II
children.text: conducting long-term health monitoring and epidemiological studies;

children.label: III
children.text: conducting long-term mental health studies; and

children.label: IV
children.text: establishing and maintaining medical surveillance programs and environmental exposure or disease registries.

children.label: i
children.text: Federal, State, and local government agencies;

children.label: ii
children.text: groups of individuals that worked or volunteered in response to the disaster in the disaster area;

children.label: iii
children.text: local residents, businesses, and schools (including parents and teachers);

children.label: iv
children.text: health care providers;

children.label: v
children.text: faith based organizations; and

children.label: vi
children.text: other organizations and persons.

children.heading: Committees
children.label: B Committees
children.text: Involvement under subparagraph (A) may be provided through the establishment of an advisory or oversight committee or board.

children.heading: Privacy
children.label: 7 Privacy
children.text: The President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, shall carry out each program under paragraph (1) in accordance with regulations relating to privacy promulgated under section 264(c) of the Health Insurance Portability and Accountability Act of 1996 ( note; ).42 U.S.C. 1320d–2Public Law 104–191

children.label: A
children.text: include the baseline clinical health examination of a participating responder under a certified monitoring programs; and1So in original. Probably should be “program;”.1

children.label: B
children.text: substitute the baseline clinical health examination of a participating responder under a certified monitoring program for a baseline clinical health examination under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Reports
children.label: c Reports
children.text: Not later than 1 year after the establishment of a program under subsection (b)(1), and every 5 years thereafter, the President, acting through the Secretary of Health and Human Services, or the medical institution or consortium of such institutions having entered into a cooperative agreement under subsection (b)(5), may submit a report to the Secretary of Homeland Security, the Secretary of Labor, the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency, and appropriate committees of Congress describing the programs and studies carried out under the program.

children.heading: National Academy of Sciences report on disaster area health and environmental protection and monitoring
children.label: d National Academy of Sciences report on disaster area health and environmental protection and monitoring

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency shall jointly enter into a contract with the National Academy of Sciences to conduct a study and prepare a report on disaster area health and environmental protection and monitoring.

children.label: A
children.text: environmental health, safety, and medicine;

children.label: B
children.text: occupational health, safety, and medicine;

children.label: C
children.text: clinical medicine, including pediatrics;

children.label: D
children.text: environmental toxicology;

children.label: E
children.text: epidemiology;

children.label: F
children.text: mental health;

children.label: G
children.text: medical monitoring and surveillance;

children.label: H
children.text: environmental monitoring and surveillance;

children.label: I
children.text: environmental and industrial hygiene;

children.label: J
children.text: emergency planning and preparedness;

children.label: K
children.text: public outreach and education;

children.label: L
children.text: State and local health departments;

children.label: M
children.text: State and local environmental protection departments;

children.label: N
children.text: functions of workers that respond to disasters, including first responders;

children.label: O
children.text: public health; and

children.label: P
children.text: family services, such as counseling and other disaster-related services provided to families.

children.label: i
children.text: chemicals or other substances for which samples should be collected in the event of a disaster, including a terrorist attack;

children.label: ii
children.text: chemical- or substance-specific methods of sample collection, including sampling methodologies and locations;

children.label: iii
children.text: chemical- or substance-specific methods of sample analysis;

children.label: iv
children.text: health-based threshold levels to be used and response actions to be taken in the event that thresholds are exceeded for individual chemicals or other substances;

children.label: I
children.text: appropriate Federal, State, and local government agencies;

children.label: II
children.text: appropriate response personnel; and

children.label: III
children.text: the public;

children.label: I
children.text: collecting and analyzing samples;

children.label: II
children.text: reporting results; and

children.label: III
children.text: taking appropriate response actions; and

children.label: vii
children.text: capabilities and capacity within the Federal Government to conduct appropriate environmental monitoring and response in the event of a disaster, including a terrorist attack; and

children.label: B
children.text: other issues specified by the Secretary of Health and Human Services, the Secretary of Homeland Security, and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 4 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There are authorized to be appropriated such sums as are necessary to carry out this subsection.
